name: Khashoggi Murder
description: The Khashoggi Murder refers to the killing of Jamal Khashoggi, a Saudi Arabian journalist and Washington Post columnist, on October 2, 2018, inside the Saudi consulate in Istanbul, Turkey. Mainstream consensus, based on intelligence assessments and investigations by various governments and organizations, points to the direct involvement of Saudi government officials, with strong implications suggesting orders originated from high levels of the Saudi leadership. The Saudi government initially denied any knowledge of Khashoggi's whereabouts, then offered conflicting explanations, eventually acknowledging that he died inside the consulate but claiming it was the result of a 'rogue operation'. Proponents of various theories suggest the murder was a meticulously planned assassination, possibly involving a body double, designed to silence a prominent critic of the Saudi regime and send a chilling message to other dissidents. Evidence cited includes leaked audio recordings from inside the consulate, witness testimony, and the movements of a Saudi hit team. Some theories explore potential international collaborations or cover-ups. Mainstream perspectives focus on the Saudi government's culpability and the geopolitical implications of the event, emphasizing the need for accountability and transparency. Critics point to the Saudi government's shifting narratives and lack of full cooperation with international investigations as evidence of a cover-up. Alternative theories suggest that the operation was initiated without direct orders from the Crown Prince but was intended to please him, or that other actors were involved to deliberately damage the Saudi government's reputation. The event has sparked global outrage and scrutiny of Saudi Arabia's human rights record and its relationship with Western powers.
subtitle: The killing of Jamal Khashoggi.

claims.claim: The Saudi government directly ordered Khashoggi's assassination.
claims.evidence: Intelligence assessments, audio recordings, movements of Saudi team.
claims.criticism: Saudi government denies direct order from the Crown Prince; claims rogue operation.

claims.claim: The murder was a pre-planned operation, not an accidental killing.
claims.evidence: Pre-planning of the team's arrival, forensic expert, body double theories.
claims.criticism: Saudi claims of spontaneous violence; lack of irrefutable direct order evidence.

claims.claim: The Saudi government is actively covering up the full truth of the murder.
claims.evidence: Conflicting narratives, lack of full cooperation, hindering international investigations.
claims.criticism: Saudi government's initial investigations; prosecution of some individuals involved.

keyFigures.name: Mohammed bin Salman
keyFigures.role: Saudi Crown Prince, implicated in the murder

keyFigures.name: Agnes Callamard
keyFigures.role: UN Special Rapporteur, investigated the murder

mythologyConnections: None directly, but echoes themes of power, betrayal, and silencing dissent found in various mythologies

timeline.event: Jamal Khashoggi enters the Saudi consulate in Istanbul.
Saudi Arabia admits Khashoggi died in the consulate.
UN report concludes Khashoggi's death was an 'extrajudicial killing' for which the Saudi state was responsible.
Saudi court sentences eight people to prison for Khashoggi's murder.

sources.author: Agnes Callamard
sources.work: Report of the Special Rapporteur on extrajudicial, summary or arbitrary executions on the mission to Turkey regarding the murder of Jamal Khashoggi
sources.title: Agnes Callamard

sources.author: Bruce Riedel
sources.work: Kings and Presidents: Saudi Arabia and the United States Since FDR
sources.title: Bruce Riedel
